title: Insights from ClinicalTrials.gov: Mining Protein Mutations and RSids
author: Shray Alag
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bfacjiaw
materials: HPO - Human Phenotype Ontology HPO is a standardized vocabulary of phenotype abnormalities that are seen in humans. HPO is a product of the Monarch Initiative and one of the thirteen driver projects in the Global Alliance for Genomics and Health strategic roadmap. The HPO ontology files are available in the OBO flat-file format and are easy to read and parse. HPO annotations provide a correlation between HPO terms and genes. There are three annotation files that contain associations between genes and phenotypes. The HPO files used in this project consisted of 14,961 HPO nodes, with 18,547 parent-child relationships between the nodes. Furthermore, 820,297 gene-phenotype annotations mapped across 4,312 unique genes and 8,947 individual HPO terms. Human Phenotype Ontology -- HPO Ontology https://hpo.jax.org/app/ Disease terms with associated associations to genes Ontology download OBO flat file http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/hp.obo Annotation download https://hpo.jax.org/app/download/annotation

steps.title: Two publicly-available datasets were used in this study
steps.instruction: Two publicly-available datasets were used in this study: ClinicalTrials.gov and HPO.

steps.title: Clinical Trial Data The complete repository...
steps.instruction: Clinical Trial Data The complete repository of clinical trials displayed at ClinicalTrials.gov is available in XML format with a well-defined schema. Download: https://clinicaltrials.gov/ct2/resources/download Download all clinical trials in a zip file (1.2 GB): https://clinicaltrials.gov/AllPublicXML.zip Parse XML. Schema files are below
